violations.description: Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution between uses. -Wet cloth sitting on prep table.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Front pastry case holding cream based items at 45F.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -Boards under front microwave needs painted.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Clean ice catcher shield inside big ice machine.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Clean shelving in walk in cooler. -Clean top of dishwasher.

Backflow prevention. — --Direct connection existed between the sewage system and drain originating from equipment in which food, portable equipment/utensils are placed. -Drain pipes for dishwasher need to be elevated so they are above the plane of the floor drain.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Entire kitchen needs cleaning.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. -Omelette station items need to be surrounded by ice in pan and ice needs refreshed more frequently.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Clean ice catcher guard in big ice machine.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Clean lower shelving in walk in cooler #2.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Floors, walls and ceiling on cook line, prep area and dry storage need cleaned.
Backflow prevention, back siphonage (3 pts deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Food display protection, condiment protection, clean tableware usage, sign posted (1 pt deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
